content.text:
{Do not be conformed to this world, but be transformed by the renewal of your mind.}{Romans 12:2}
 {The art of doing mathematics consists in finding that special case which contains all the germs of generality.}{— David Hilbert}

Different bases give different coordinate representations of the 
same vector. Understanding how to convert between bases is essential.

content.title: Coordinate Vector
content.content:
Let $\mathcal{B} = \{\vect{b}_1, \ldots, \vect{b}_n\}$ be a basis for 
$V$. If $\vect{x} = c_1\vect{b}_1 + \cdots + c_n\vect{b}_n$, then the 
**coordinate vector** of $\vect{x}$ 
relative to $\mathcal{B}$ is:
\[
[\vect{x}]_\mathcal{B} = \begin{bmatrix} c_1 \\ c_2 \\ \vdots \\ c_n \end{bmatrix}
\]

content.title: Coordinate vectors
content.solution:
Solve $c_1\begin{bmatrix} 1 \\ 1 \end{bmatrix} + 
c_2\begin{bmatrix} 1 \\ -1 \end{bmatrix} = \begin{bmatrix} 5 \\ 1 \end{bmatrix}$.

This gives $c_1 + c_2 = 5$ and $c_1 - c_2 = 1$, so $c_1 = 3$, $c_2 = 2$.
\[
[\vect{x}]_\mathcal{B} = \begin{bmatrix} 3 \\ 2 \end{bmatrix}
\]

content.title: Change-of-Basis Matrix
content.content:
Let $\mathcal{B}$ and $\mathcal{C}$ be bases for $V$. The 
**change-of-basis matrix** from 
$\mathcal{B}$ to $\mathcal{C}$, denoted $P_{\mathcal{C} \leftarrow \mathcal{B}}$, 
satisfies:
\[
[\vect{x}]_\mathcal{C} = P_{\mathcal{C} \leftarrow \mathcal{B}} [\vect{x}]_\mathcal{B}
\]
The columns of $P_{\mathcal{C} \leftarrow \mathcal{B}}$ are the 
$\mathcal{C}$-coordinates of the basis vectors in $\mathcal{B}$.

content.title: Change of basis in $\R^2$
content.solution:
The columns are the $\mathcal{B}$-coordinates of the $\mathcal{C}$-vectors. 
Since $\mathcal{B}$ is standard, these are just the $\mathcal{C}$-vectors:
\[
P_{\mathcal{B} \leftarrow \mathcal{C}} = 
\begin{bmatrix} 1 & 1 \\ 1 & -1 \end{bmatrix}
\]
Then $P_{\mathcal{C} \leftarrow \mathcal{B}} = (P_{\mathcal{B} \leftarrow \mathcal{C}})\inv = 
\frac{1}{-2}\begin{bmatrix} -1 & -1 \\ -1 & 1 \end{bmatrix} = 
\begin{bmatrix} 1/2 & 1/2 \\ 1/2 & -1/2 \end{bmatrix}$.

marginNotes.text:
For now we see in a mirror dimly, 
but then face to face.
